seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between East Carnarvon and East Chapman. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: East Carnarvon vs East Chapman: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in East Carnarvon and East Chapman.
heading: East Carnarvon vs East Chapman — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between East Carnarvon, Western Australia and East Chapman, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: East Chapman has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to East Carnarvon (30/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is East Carnarvon or East Chapman safer from flooding?
faq.answer: East Carnarvon has a flood risk score of 26/100, while East Chapman scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: East Carnarvon scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. East Chapman scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in East Carnarvon or East Chapman?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. East Chapman has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area face minimal climate value impact based on current projections.
